text.title: WBS Dictionary
text.summary: Structure and required fields for the WBS Dictionary; the Dictionary is what makes the numbered outline actionable.

rule.statement: Every leaf work package must have a Dictionary entry with all of these fields: ID, Name, Description (what is included AND explicitly excluded), Deliverable (the tangible output), Acceptance Criteria (observable and testable), Owner (one accountable person or named team), Effort Estimate (hours, 8-80), Dependencies (predecessor WBS IDs).
rule.rationale: Missing acceptance criteria make sign-off subjective. Missing exclusions cause scope creep. Missing ownership causes handoff gaps. A WBS tree without a Dictionary is an outline, not a scope baseline.

example.note: MVP launch WBS with PM branch and balanced depth.
example.code: MVP Launch 1.0 Project Management 1.1 Planning Documentation 1.2 Status Reporting 1.3 Risk Management 2.0 Core Features 2.1 User Authentication 2.2 Main Dashboard 2.3 Core Workflow 3.0 Quality 3.1 Test Suite 3.2 Bug Fix Cycle 4.0 Deployment 4.1 Production Deploy 4.2 Monitoring Setup 5.0 Transition 5.1 Handover Documentation 5.2 Support Handover

example.note: Dictionary entry for a single leaf work package.
example.code:
## WBS 2.1.1 — Interview 10 Users
| Field | Value | |---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------| | Parent | 2.1 User Research | | Description | 30-min structured interviews; excludes survey analysis | | Deliverable | Interview transcripts + synthesis report | | Acceptance Criteria | 10 interviews completed; notes documented and reviewed | | Owner | UX Researcher | | Effort Estimate | 20 hours | | Dependencies | 2.1.0 Interview guide approved |

antipattern.description: WBS tree without a Dictionary — just numbered labels.
antipattern.reason: Numbered labels without definitions leave ownership, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries undefined. The PM interprets everything; the team guesses.

antipattern.description: Dates or assignees in the WBS tree structure.
antipattern.reason: The WBS answers "what"; the schedule answers "when"; the RACI answers "who". Mixing them forces WBS changes every time the schedule shifts.

text.reference: YAML validator for 100% rule and 8/80 rule
